sections.title: Key Risks
sections.content:
• Tariffs and trade measures could materially impact supply chain, costs, and consumer demand. (Evidence: Beginning in the second quarter of 2025, new U.S. Tariffs were announced, including additional tariffs on imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam and the EU... Tariffs and other)
• Foreign exchange rate fluctuations may adversely affect net sales, gross margins, and the value of investments. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to foreign exchange rate risk relates primarily to the Company being a net receiver of currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Changes in exchange rates, and in particular a stre)
• Regulatory investigations and legal proceedings, particularly in the EU under the Digital Markets Act, could result in significant fines and operational changes. (Evidence: On April 23, 2025, the Commission fined the Company €500 million in the Article 5(4) Investigation and issued a cease and desist order... If the Commission makes a final determination in the Article 6)
• Credit risk on trade receivables and vendor non-trade receivables is heightened, especially with concentration among cellular carriers and Asian vendors. (Evidence: A substantial majority of the Company’s outstanding trade receivables are not covered by collateral... and a significant portion of the Company’s trade receivables can be concentrated within cellular )
• Changes in tax laws, including global minimum tax standards, could increase the Company's effective tax rate and tax liabilities. (Evidence: Due to economic and political conditions, tax laws and tax rates for income taxes and other non-income taxes in various jurisdictions may be subject to significant change. For example, the Organisatio)

sections.title: Financial Overview
sections.content:
• Total Net Sales: $416.2B (vs. $391.0B) — +6.4% — Growth driven by Services (+13.5%) and iPhone (+4.2%), partially offset by Wearables, Home and Accessories (-3.6%).
• Gross Margin: $195.2B (46.9%) (vs. $180.7B (46.2%)) — +8.0% (+0.7 pts) — Products gross margin percentage declined to 36.8% from 37.2% due to mix and tariffs; Services margin expanded to 75.4% from 73.9%.
• Operating Income: $133.1B (32.0% margin) (vs. $123.2B (31.5% margin)) — +8.0% (+0.5 pts) — Operating expenses grew 8.2% to $62.2B, with R&D up 10.1% and SG&A up 5.8%.
• Net Income: $112.0B (26.9% margin) (vs. $93.7B (24.0% margin)) — +19.5% (+2.9 pts) — Effective tax rate dropped to 15.6% from 24.1%, primarily due to a $10.7B decrease in provision related to the State Aid Decision.
• Diluted EPS: $7.46 (vs. $6.08) — +22.7% — Share count declined 2.6% to 15.0B diluted shares, amplifying EPS growth.
• Operating Cash Flow: $111.5B (vs. $118.3B) — -5.7% — Decrease driven by higher cash paid for income taxes ($43.4B vs. $26.1B) and working capital changes.
• Free Cash Flow: $98.8B (vs. $108.8B) — -9.2% — Capex increased 34.6% to $12.7B, reflecting investments in manufacturing and data centers.
• Total Assets: $359.2B (vs. $365.0B) — -1.6% — Decline in marketable securities partially offset by higher cash and other non-current assets.
• Cash & Equivalents: $35.9B (vs. $29.9B) — +20.0% — Increase reflects net cash from operations and investing activities, partially offset by shareholder returns.
• Total Debt: $98.7B (vs. $106.6B) — -7.5% — Includes $12.4B current term debt, $78.3B non-current term debt, and $8.0B commercial paper.

sections.title: Management Commentary
sections.content:
Themes: - Services growth remains the primary driver of revenue expansion, with advertising, App Store, and cloud services leading the 14% increase.
- iPhone revenue growth was modest at 4%, driven by higher sales of Pro models, while Greater China sales declined 4% due to lower iPhone sales.
- Gross margin benefited from favorable costs and Services mix, but tariffs and product mix pressured Products margin.
- The effective tax rate dropped significantly to 15.6% due to a $10.7B reduction in provision related to the State Aid Decision and changes in unrecognized tax benefits.
- Capital expenditures increased 35% to $12.7B, reflecting investments in manufacturing and data centers to support product and service expansion.
Quotes: - Speaker: Management (MD&A)
Quote: The Company believes its balances of cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, which totaled $132.4 billion as of September 27, 2025, along with cash generated by ongoing operations and continued access to debt markets, will be sufficient to satisfy its cash requirements and capital return program over the next 12 months and beyond.
Context: Liquidity and Capital Resources discussion, affirming strong financial position.
- Speaker: Management (MD&A)
Quote: The Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure.
Context: Forward-looking caution on gross margin sustainability amid tariff and competitive pressures.
Capital Allocation: - Share repurchases totaled $89.3B in FY2025, with a new $100B program authorized in May 2025; $99.8B remained available as of September 27, 2025.
- Dividends per share increased 4% to $1.02 annually, with total dividend payments of $15.4B.
- Capital expenditures rose to $12.7B, up from $9.4B, focused on manufacturing and data center investments.
- Debt repayment of $10.9B in term debt and $2.0B net reduction in commercial paper, reducing total debt by $7.9B.
Source Section Ref: Item 7. MD&A; Item 8. Financial Statements

sections.title: Strategic Developments
sections.content:
Guidance: Not disclosed—the company did not provide specific quantitative guidance in the 10-K filing.
Tone: cautious
Drivers: - Management expects gross margins to face volatility and downward pressure due to tariffs, product mix, and foreign exchange.
- Services growth expected to continue, driven by advertising, App Store, and cloud services.
- Ongoing investments in R&D and infrastructure to support product innovation and services expansion.
Watch Items: - Impact of U.S. tariffs and potential retaliatory measures on supply chain and costs.
- Resolution of EU DMA investigations and potential fines.
- Outcome of DOJ antitrust lawsuit and Epic Games appeal.
- Changes in global tax regulations, including OECD global minimum tax.
Source Section Ref: Item 7. MD&A (Gross Margin discussion); Item 1A. Risk Factors

summary.business_overview:
*Auto-generated from structured data because the writer output failed validation (Editorial summary length must be 100-320 words (current: 618).).*
## Executive Summary
Apple reports record revenue of $416.2B in FY2025, up 6.4% YoY, driven by 14% growth in Services; net income rises 19.5% to $112.0B, aided by lower effective tax rate. Management's disclosed tone was positive. Total net sales increased 6.4% to $416.2B, with Services revenue up 13.5% to $109.2B, now 26.2% of total sales.; Net income grew 19.5% to $112.0B, with diluted EPS of $7.46, up 22.7% YoY, benefiting from a lower effective tax rate of 15.6% vs. 24.1% in 2024.; Operating cash flow declined 5.7% to $111.5B, while free cash flow fell 9.2% to $98.8B, reflecting higher capex and working capital changes.; The company returned $104.7B to shareholders through $89.3B in share repurchases and $15.4B in dividends, and announced a new $100B buyback program.

## Financials
- Total Net Sales: $416.2B vs. $391.0B (+6.4%) – Growth driven by Services (+13.5%) and iPhone (+4.2%), partially offset by Wearables, Home and Accessories (-3.6%).
- Gross Margin: $195.2B (46.9%) vs. $180.7B (46.2%) (+8.0% (+0.7 pts)) – Products gross margin percentage declined to 36.8% from 37.2% due to mix and tariffs; Services margin expanded to 75.4% from 73.9%.
- Operating Income: $133.1B (32.0% margin) vs. $123.2B (31.5% margin) (+8.0% (+0.5 pts)) – Operating expenses grew 8.2% to $62.2B, with R&D up 10.1% and SG&A up 5.8%.
- Net Income: $112.0B (26.9% margin) vs. $93.7B (24.0% margin) (+19.5% (+2.9 pts)) – Effective tax rate dropped to 15.6% from 24.1%, primarily due to a $10.7B decrease in provision related to the State Aid Decision.
- Diluted EPS: $7.46 vs. $6.08 (+22.7%) – Share count declined 2.6% to 15.0B diluted shares, amplifying EPS growth.
- Operating Cash Flow: $111.5B vs. $118.3B (-5.7%) – Decrease driven by higher cash paid for income taxes ($43.4B vs. $26.1B) and working capital changes.
- Free Cash Flow: $98.8B vs. $108.8B (-9.2%) – Capex increased 34.6% to $12.7B, reflecting investments in manufacturing and data centers.
- Total Assets: $359.2B vs. $365.0B (-1.6%) – Decline in marketable securities partially offset by higher cash and other non-current assets.
- Cash & Equivalents: $35.9B vs. $29.9B (+20.0%) – Increase reflects net cash from operations and investing activities, partially offset by shareholder returns.
- Total Debt: $98.7B vs. $106.6B (-7.5%) – Includes $12.4B current term debt, $78.3B non-current term debt, and $8.0B commercial paper.
- Profitability: Gross margin expanded 0.7 pts to 46.9%, with Services gross margin reaching 75.4% (+1.5 pts) and Products gross margin declining to 36.8% (-0.4 pts) due to tariff costs and product mix.; Operating margin improved to 32.0% from 31.5%, despite a 10.1% increase in R&D expense, reflecting operating leverage on higher sales.; Net margin surged to 26.9% from 24.0%, primarily due to a lower effective tax rate of 15.6% vs. 24.1% in the prior year.
- Cash flow: Operating cash flow decreased 5.7% to $111.5B, with net income growth more than offset by a $17.3B increase in cash taxes paid and unfavorable working capital movements.; Free cash flow fell 9.2% to $98.8B, as capital expenditures rose 34.6% to $12.7B, reflecting increased investment in manufacturing and data center infrastructure.; Cash generated by investing activities was $15.2B, driven by net maturities of marketable securities, partially offset by capex.
- Balance sheet: Total assets declined 1.6% to $359.2B, with marketable securities decreasing to $96.5B from $126.7B, while cash and equivalents increased to $35.9B.; Total debt decreased 7.5% to $98.7B, with term debt of $90.7B and commercial paper of $8.0B; net cash position (cash + marketable securities less total debt) was $33.7B.; Shareholders' equity increased 29.5% to $73.7B, driven by net income and share-based compensation, partially offset by $90.1B in share repurchases and $15.4B in dividends.

## Risks
- Tariffs and trade measures could materially impact supply chain, costs, and consumer demand. (Evidence: Beginning in the second quarter of 2025, new U.S. Tariffs were announced, including additional tariffs on imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam and the EU... Tariffs and other measures that are applied to the Company’s products or their components can have a material adverse impact on the Company’s business, results of operations and financial condition, including impacting the Company’s supply chain, the availability of rare earths and other raw materials and components, pricing and gross margin.)
- Foreign exchange rate fluctuations may adversely affect net sales, gross margins, and the value of investments. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to foreign exchange rate risk relates primarily to the Company being a net receiver of currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Changes in exchange rates, and in particular a strengthening of the U.S. dollar, will negatively affect the Company’s net sales and gross margins as expressed in U.S. dollars.)
- Regulatory investigations and legal proceedings, particularly in the EU under the Digital Markets Act, could result in significant fines and operational changes. (Evidence: On April 23, 2025, the Commission fined the Company €500 million in the Article 5(4) Investigation and issued a cease and desist order... If the Commission makes a final determination in the Article 6(4) Investigation that there has been a violation, it can issue a cease and desist order and may impose fines up to 10% of the Company’s annual worldwide net sales.)
- Credit risk on trade receivables and vendor non-trade receivables is heightened, especially with concentration among cellular carriers and Asian vendors. (Evidence: A substantial majority of the Company’s outstanding trade receivables are not covered by collateral... and a significant portion of the Company’s trade receivables can be concentrated within cellular network carriers or other resellers. As of September 27, 2025, the Company’s vendor non-trade receivables were concentrated among a few individual vendors located primarily in Asia.)
- Changes in tax laws, including global minimum tax standards, could increase the Company's effective tax rate and tax liabilities. (Evidence: Due to economic and political conditions, tax laws and tax rates for income taxes and other non-income taxes in various jurisdictions may be subject to significant change. For example, the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development continues to advance proposals for modernizing international tax rules, including the introduction of global minimum tax standards.)

## Management Commentary
- Themes: Services growth remains the primary driver of revenue expansion, with advertising, App Store, and cloud services leading the 14% increase.; iPhone revenue growth was modest at 4%, driven by higher sales of Pro models, while Greater China sales declined 4% due to lower iPhone sales.; Gross margin benefited from favorable costs and Services mix, but tariffs and product mix pressured Products margin.; The effective tax rate dropped significantly to 15.6% due to a $10.7B reduction in provision related to the State Aid Decision and changes in unrecognized tax benefits.; Capital expenditures increased 35% to $12.7B, reflecting investments in manufacturing and data centers to support product and service expansion.
- Capital allocation: Share repurchases totaled $89.3B in FY2025, with a new $100B program authorized in May 2025; $99.8B remained available as of September 27, 2025.; Dividends per share increased 4% to $1.02 annually, with total dividend payments of $15.4B.; Capital expenditures rose to $12.7B, up from $9.4B, focused on manufacturing and data center investments.; Debt repayment of $10.9B in term debt and $2.0B net reduction in commercial paper, reducing total debt by $7.9B.
> "The Company believes its balances of cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, which totaled $132.4 billion as of September 27, 2025, along with cash generated by ongoing operations and continued access to debt markets, will be sufficient to satisfy its cash requirements and capital return program over the next 12 months and beyond." – Management (MD&A)
> "The Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure." – Management (MD&A)

## Outlook
- Guidance: Not disclosed—the company did not provide specific quantitative guidance in the 10-K filing.
- Tone: cautious
- Drivers: Management expects gross margins to face volatility and downward pressure due to tariffs, product mix, and foreign exchange.; Services growth expected to continue, driven by advertising, App Store, and cloud services.; Ongoing investments in R&D and infrastructure to support product innovation and services expansion.
- Watch items: Impact of U.S. tariffs and potential retaliatory measures on supply chain and costs.; Resolution of EU DMA investigations and potential fines.; Outcome of DOJ antitrust lawsuit and Epic Games appeal.; Changes in global tax regulations, including OECD global minimum tax.

quotes.speaker: Management (MD&A)
quotes.quote: The Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure.
quotes.context: Forward-looking caution on gross margin sustainability amid tariff and competitive pressures.

capital_allocation: Share repurchases totaled $89.3B in FY2025, with a new $100B program authorized in May 2025; $99.8B remained available as of September 27, 2025.
Dividends per share increased 4% to $1.02 annually, with total dividend payments of $15.4B.
Capital expenditures rose to $12.7B, up from $9.4B, focused on manufacturing and data center investments.
Debt repayment of $10.9B in term debt and $2.0B net reduction in commercial paper, reducing total debt by $7.9B.

segment_performance.commentary: Growth driven by higher iPhone and Services sales; unfavorable currency impact partially offset.
Higher sales of Services, iPhone, and Mac drove growth.
Decline due to lower iPhone sales, partially offset by higher Mac sales.
Strong growth from iPhone, Services, and iPad.
Higher sales of iPhone, Services, and Mac.

liquidity_capital_structure.leverage: Total debt of $98.7B, with net cash position (cash + marketable securities less total debt) of $33.7B; debt-to-equity ratio of 1.34x, down from 1.87x in 2024.
liquidity_capital_structure.liquidity: Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $132.4B, with $35.9B in cash and equivalents; operating cash flow of $111.5B provides ample liquidity.
shareholder_returns: Repurchased $89.3B of common stock, reducing diluted share count by 2.6%.
Paid $15.4B in dividends and dividend equivalents, with quarterly dividend raised to $0.26 per share.
New $100B share repurchase program authorized in May 2025, with $99.8B remaining.
liquidity_capital_structure.source_section_ref: Item 7. MD&A (Liquidity and Capital Resources); Item 8. Balance Sheet

debt_covenants: Not disclosed—no specific debt covenant violations or restrictive covenants highlighted in the filing.
contingent_liabilities: EU Digital Markets Act: €500 million fine imposed in April 2025 for Article 5(4) violation; appeal filed. Potential additional fines up to 10% of annual worldwide net sales for Article 6(4) investigation.
DOJ antitrust lawsuit filed March 2024 alleging monopolization of smartphone markets; various civil litigation matters seeking damages.
Epic Games: California District Court found violation of 2021 Injunction, enjoining commissions on out-of-app purchases; appeal pending, with potential criminal contempt referral.
Deemed repatriation tax payable of $8.8B under TCJA, due within 12 months.
covenants_contingencies.source_section_ref: Item 3. Legal Proceedings; Item 7. MD&A (Contractual Obligations)

guidance_outlook.guidance: Not disclosed—the company did not provide specific quantitative guidance in the 10-K filing.
drivers: Management expects gross margins to face volatility and downward pressure due to tariffs, product mix, and foreign exchange.
Services growth expected to continue, driven by advertising, App Store, and cloud services.
Ongoing investments in R&D and infrastructure to support product innovation and services expansion.
watch_items: Impact of U.S. tariffs and potential retaliatory measures on supply chain and costs.
Resolution of EU DMA investigations and potential fines.
Outcome of DOJ antitrust lawsuit and Epic Games appeal.
Changes in global tax regulations, including OECD global minimum tax.
guidance_outlook.source_section_ref: Item 7. MD&A (Gross Margin discussion); Item 1A. Risk Factors

notable_footnotes.item: State Aid Decision impact on tax provision
notable_footnotes.impact: Provision for income taxes decreased by $10.7B YoY, primarily due to a one-time adjustment related to the State Aid Decision, lowering the effective tax rate to 15.6%.
Enhanced segment disclosures, including significant segment expenses regularly provided to the chief operating decision maker, adopted in 2025 annual reporting.
notable_footnotes.source_section_ref: Note 1 – Summary of Significant Accounting Policies

notable_footnotes.impact: Total manufacturing purchase obligations of $56.2B, with $55.4B payable within 12 months, reflecting significant supply chain commitments.

three_year_trend.trend_summary: Apple demonstrated consistent revenue growth over three years, with total net sales increasing from $383.3B in 2023 to $416.2B in 2025, a CAGR of 4.2%. Net income recovered from a dip in 2024 to reach a record $112.0B in 2025, driven by Services expansion and a lower tax rate. Gross margins improved steadily from 44.1% to 46.9%, while operating cash flow remained robust above $110B annually.
inflections: Services revenue surpassed $100B for the first time in 2025, growing at a 13-14% annual rate and now representing over 26% of total sales.
Net income surged 19.5% in 2025 after a 3.4% decline in 2024, due to a sharp drop in the effective tax rate from 24.1% to 15.6%.
Free cash flow declined in 2025 to $98.8B from $108.8B in 2024, as capex increased 35% and cash taxes paid rose 66%.
Greater China revenue declined for the second consecutive year, down 4% in 2025 after an 8% drop in 2024, contrasting with growth in all other regions.

insights: Revenue growth accelerated to 6.4% in 2025 from 2.0% in 2024, driven by stronger iPhone and Services performance.
Operating margin expanded 0.5 pts to 32.0%, compared to a 1.8 pt expansion in 2024, as operating expense growth outpaced revenue growth.
Share repurchases remained elevated at $89-95B annually, reducing diluted share count by 2.6-2.8% each year.

three_year_trend.source_section_ref: Item 8. Financial Statements (3-year data); Item 7. MD&A

executive_snapshot.headline: Apple reports record revenue of $416.2B in FY2025, up 6.4% YoY, driven by 14% growth in Services; net income rises 19.5% to $112.0B, aided by lower effective tax rate.
key_points: Total net sales increased 6.4% to $416.2B, with Services revenue up 13.5% to $109.2B, now 26.2% of total sales.
Net income grew 19.5% to $112.0B, with diluted EPS of $7.46, up 22.7% YoY, benefiting from a lower effective tax rate of 15.6% vs. 24.1% in 2024.
Operating cash flow declined 5.7% to $111.5B, while free cash flow fell 9.2% to $98.8B, reflecting higher capex and working capital changes.
The company returned $104.7B to shareholders through $89.3B in share repurchases and $15.4B in dividends, and announced a new $100B buyback program.
executive_snapshot.source_section_ref: Item 7. Management’s Discussion and Analysis; Item 8. Financial Statements

table.commentary: Growth driven by Services (+13.5%) and iPhone (+4.2%), partially offset by Wearables, Home and Accessories (-3.6%).
Products gross margin percentage declined to 36.8% from 37.2% due to mix and tariffs; Services margin expanded to 75.4% from 73.9%.
Operating expenses grew 8.2% to $62.2B, with R&D up 10.1% and SG&A up 5.8%.
Effective tax rate dropped to 15.6% from 24.1%, primarily due to a $10.7B decrease in provision related to the State Aid Decision.
Share count declined 2.6% to 15.0B diluted shares, amplifying EPS growth.
Decrease driven by higher cash paid for income taxes ($43.4B vs. $26.1B) and working capital changes.
Capex increased 34.6% to $12.7B, reflecting investments in manufacturing and data centers.
Decline in marketable securities partially offset by higher cash and other non-current assets.
Increase reflects net cash from operations and investing activities, partially offset by shareholder returns.
Includes $12.4B current term debt, $78.3B non-current term debt, and $8.0B commercial paper.

profitability: Gross margin expanded 0.7 pts to 46.9%, with Services gross margin reaching 75.4% (+1.5 pts) and Products gross margin declining to 36.8% (-0.4 pts) due to tariff costs and product mix.
Operating margin improved to 32.0% from 31.5%, despite a 10.1% increase in R&D expense, reflecting operating leverage on higher sales.
Net margin surged to 26.9% from 24.0%, primarily due to a lower effective tax rate of 15.6% vs. 24.1% in the prior year.
cash_flow: Operating cash flow decreased 5.7% to $111.5B, with net income growth more than offset by a $17.3B increase in cash taxes paid and unfavorable working capital movements.
Free cash flow fell 9.2% to $98.8B, as capital expenditures rose 34.6% to $12.7B, reflecting increased investment in manufacturing and data center infrastructure.
Cash generated by investing activities was $15.2B, driven by net maturities of marketable securities, partially offset by capex.
balance_sheet: Total assets declined 1.6% to $359.2B, with marketable securities decreasing to $96.5B from $126.7B, while cash and equivalents increased to $35.9B.
Total debt decreased 7.5% to $98.7B, with term debt of $90.7B and commercial paper of $8.0B; net cash position (cash + marketable securities less total debt) was $33.7B.
Shareholders' equity increased 29.5% to $73.7B, driven by net income and share-based compensation, partially offset by $90.1B in share repurchases and $15.4B in dividends.

risk_factors.summary: Tariffs and trade measures could materially impact supply chain, costs, and consumer demand.
risk_factors.supporting_evidence: Beginning in the second quarter of 2025, new U.S. Tariffs were announced, including additional tariffs on imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam and the EU... Tariffs and other measures that are applied to the Company’s products or their components can have a material adverse impact on the Company’s business, results of operations and financial condition, including impacting the Company’s supply chain, the availability of rare earths and other raw materials and components, pricing and gross margin.

risk_factors.summary: Foreign exchange rate fluctuations may adversely affect net sales, gross margins, and the value of investments.
risk_factors.supporting_evidence: The Company’s exposure to foreign exchange rate risk relates primarily to the Company being a net receiver of currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Changes in exchange rates, and in particular a strengthening of the U.S. dollar, will negatively affect the Company’s net sales and gross margins as expressed in U.S. dollars.

risk_factors.summary: Regulatory investigations and legal proceedings, particularly in the EU under the Digital Markets Act, could result in significant fines and operational changes.
risk_factors.supporting_evidence: On April 23, 2025, the Commission fined the Company €500 million in the Article 5(4) Investigation and issued a cease and desist order... If the Commission makes a final determination in the Article 6(4) Investigation that there has been a violation, it can issue a cease and desist order and may impose fines up to 10% of the Company’s annual worldwide net sales.

risk_factors.summary: Credit risk on trade receivables and vendor non-trade receivables is heightened, especially with concentration among cellular carriers and Asian vendors.
risk_factors.supporting_evidence: A substantial majority of the Company’s outstanding trade receivables are not covered by collateral... and a significant portion of the Company’s trade receivables can be concentrated within cellular network carriers or other resellers. As of September 27, 2025, the Company’s vendor non-trade receivables were concentrated among a few individual vendors located primarily in Asia.

risk_factors.summary: Changes in tax laws, including global minimum tax standards, could increase the Company's effective tax rate and tax liabilities.
risk_factors.supporting_evidence: Due to economic and political conditions, tax laws and tax rates for income taxes and other non-income taxes in various jurisdictions may be subject to significant change. For example, the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development continues to advance proposals for modernizing international tax rules, including the introduction of global minimum tax standards.

themes: Services growth remains the primary driver of revenue expansion, with advertising, App Store, and cloud services leading the 14% increase.
iPhone revenue growth was modest at 4%, driven by higher sales of Pro models, while Greater China sales declined 4% due to lower iPhone sales.
Gross margin benefited from favorable costs and Services mix, but tariffs and product mix pressured Products margin.
The effective tax rate dropped significantly to 15.6% due to a $10.7B reduction in provision related to the State Aid Decision and changes in unrecognized tax benefits.
Capital expenditures increased 35% to $12.7B, reflecting investments in manufacturing and data centers to support product and service expansion.

writer.markdown:
*Auto-generated from structured data because the writer output failed validation (Editorial summary length must be 100-320 words (current: 618).).*
## Executive Summary
Apple reports record revenue of $416.2B in FY2025, up 6.4% YoY, driven by 14% growth in Services; net income rises 19.5% to $112.0B, aided by lower effective tax rate. Management's disclosed tone was positive. Total net sales increased 6.4% to $416.2B, with Services revenue up 13.5% to $109.2B, now 26.2% of total sales.; Net income grew 19.5% to $112.0B, with diluted EPS of $7.46, up 22.7% YoY, benefiting from a lower effective tax rate of 15.6% vs. 24.1% in 2024.; Operating cash flow declined 5.7% to $111.5B, while free cash flow fell 9.2% to $98.8B, reflecting higher capex and working capital changes.; The company returned $104.7B to shareholders through $89.3B in share repurchases and $15.4B in dividends, and announced a new $100B buyback program.

## Financials
- Total Net Sales: $416.2B vs. $391.0B (+6.4%) – Growth driven by Services (+13.5%) and iPhone (+4.2%), partially offset by Wearables, Home and Accessories (-3.6%).
- Gross Margin: $195.2B (46.9%) vs. $180.7B (46.2%) (+8.0% (+0.7 pts)) – Products gross margin percentage declined to 36.8% from 37.2% due to mix and tariffs; Services margin expanded to 75.4% from 73.9%.
- Operating Income: $133.1B (32.0% margin) vs. $123.2B (31.5% margin) (+8.0% (+0.5 pts)) – Operating expenses grew 8.2% to $62.2B, with R&D up 10.1% and SG&A up 5.8%.
- Net Income: $112.0B (26.9% margin) vs. $93.7B (24.0% margin) (+19.5% (+2.9 pts)) – Effective tax rate dropped to 15.6% from 24.1%, primarily due to a $10.7B decrease in provision related to the State Aid Decision.
- Diluted EPS: $7.46 vs. $6.08 (+22.7%) – Share count declined 2.6% to 15.0B diluted shares, amplifying EPS growth.
- Operating Cash Flow: $111.5B vs. $118.3B (-5.7%) – Decrease driven by higher cash paid for income taxes ($43.4B vs. $26.1B) and working capital changes.
- Free Cash Flow: $98.8B vs. $108.8B (-9.2%) – Capex increased 34.6% to $12.7B, reflecting investments in manufacturing and data centers.
- Total Assets: $359.2B vs. $365.0B (-1.6%) – Decline in marketable securities partially offset by higher cash and other non-current assets.
- Cash & Equivalents: $35.9B vs. $29.9B (+20.0%) – Increase reflects net cash from operations and investing activities, partially offset by shareholder returns.
- Total Debt: $98.7B vs. $106.6B (-7.5%) – Includes $12.4B current term debt, $78.3B non-current term debt, and $8.0B commercial paper.
- Profitability: Gross margin expanded 0.7 pts to 46.9%, with Services gross margin reaching 75.4% (+1.5 pts) and Products gross margin declining to 36.8% (-0.4 pts) due to tariff costs and product mix.; Operating margin improved to 32.0% from 31.5%, despite a 10.1% increase in R&D expense, reflecting operating leverage on higher sales.; Net margin surged to 26.9% from 24.0%, primarily due to a lower effective tax rate of 15.6% vs. 24.1% in the prior year.
- Cash flow: Operating cash flow decreased 5.7% to $111.5B, with net income growth more than offset by a $17.3B increase in cash taxes paid and unfavorable working capital movements.; Free cash flow fell 9.2% to $98.8B, as capital expenditures rose 34.6% to $12.7B, reflecting increased investment in manufacturing and data center infrastructure.; Cash generated by investing activities was $15.2B, driven by net maturities of marketable securities, partially offset by capex.
- Balance sheet: Total assets declined 1.6% to $359.2B, with marketable securities decreasing to $96.5B from $126.7B, while cash and equivalents increased to $35.9B.; Total debt decreased 7.5% to $98.7B, with term debt of $90.7B and commercial paper of $8.0B; net cash position (cash + marketable securities less total debt) was $33.7B.; Shareholders' equity increased 29.5% to $73.7B, driven by net income and share-based compensation, partially offset by $90.1B in share repurchases and $15.4B in dividends.

## Risks
- Tariffs and trade measures could materially impact supply chain, costs, and consumer demand. (Evidence: Beginning in the second quarter of 2025, new U.S. Tariffs were announced, including additional tariffs on imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam and the EU... Tariffs and other measures that are applied to the Company’s products or their components can have a material adverse impact on the Company’s business, results of operations and financial condition, including impacting the Company’s supply chain, the availability of rare earths and other raw materials and components, pricing and gross margin.)
- Foreign exchange rate fluctuations may adversely affect net sales, gross margins, and the value of investments. (Evidence: The Company’s exposure to foreign exchange rate risk relates primarily to the Company being a net receiver of currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Changes in exchange rates, and in particular a strengthening of the U.S. dollar, will negatively affect the Company’s net sales and gross margins as expressed in U.S. dollars.)
- Regulatory investigations and legal proceedings, particularly in the EU under the Digital Markets Act, could result in significant fines and operational changes. (Evidence: On April 23, 2025, the Commission fined the Company €500 million in the Article 5(4) Investigation and issued a cease and desist order... If the Commission makes a final determination in the Article 6(4) Investigation that there has been a violation, it can issue a cease and desist order and may impose fines up to 10% of the Company’s annual worldwide net sales.)
- Credit risk on trade receivables and vendor non-trade receivables is heightened, especially with concentration among cellular carriers and Asian vendors. (Evidence: A substantial majority of the Company’s outstanding trade receivables are not covered by collateral... and a significant portion of the Company’s trade receivables can be concentrated within cellular network carriers or other resellers. As of September 27, 2025, the Company’s vendor non-trade receivables were concentrated among a few individual vendors located primarily in Asia.)
- Changes in tax laws, including global minimum tax standards, could increase the Company's effective tax rate and tax liabilities. (Evidence: Due to economic and political conditions, tax laws and tax rates for income taxes and other non-income taxes in various jurisdictions may be subject to significant change. For example, the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development continues to advance proposals for modernizing international tax rules, including the introduction of global minimum tax standards.)

## Management Commentary
- Themes: Services growth remains the primary driver of revenue expansion, with advertising, App Store, and cloud services leading the 14% increase.; iPhone revenue growth was modest at 4%, driven by higher sales of Pro models, while Greater China sales declined 4% due to lower iPhone sales.; Gross margin benefited from favorable costs and Services mix, but tariffs and product mix pressured Products margin.; The effective tax rate dropped significantly to 15.6% due to a $10.7B reduction in provision related to the State Aid Decision and changes in unrecognized tax benefits.; Capital expenditures increased 35% to $12.7B, reflecting investments in manufacturing and data centers to support product and service expansion.
- Capital allocation: Share repurchases totaled $89.3B in FY2025, with a new $100B program authorized in May 2025; $99.8B remained available as of September 27, 2025.; Dividends per share increased 4% to $1.02 annually, with total dividend payments of $15.4B.; Capital expenditures rose to $12.7B, up from $9.4B, focused on manufacturing and data center investments.; Debt repayment of $10.9B in term debt and $2.0B net reduction in commercial paper, reducing total debt by $7.9B.
> "The Company believes its balances of cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, which totaled $132.4 billion as of September 27, 2025, along with cash generated by ongoing operations and continued access to debt markets, will be sufficient to satisfy its cash requirements and capital return program over the next 12 months and beyond." – Management (MD&A)
> "The Company believes, in general, gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure." – Management (MD&A)

## Outlook
- Guidance: Not disclosed—the company did not provide specific quantitative guidance in the 10-K filing.
- Tone: cautious
- Drivers: Management expects gross margins to face volatility and downward pressure due to tariffs, product mix, and foreign exchange.; Services growth expected to continue, driven by advertising, App Store, and cloud services.; Ongoing investments in R&D and infrastructure to support product innovation and services expansion.
- Watch items: Impact of U.S. tariffs and potential retaliatory measures on supply chain and costs.; Resolution of EU DMA investigations and potential fines.; Outcome of DOJ antitrust lawsuit and Epic Games appeal.; Changes in global tax regulations, including OECD global minimum tax.
